source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
The sum the dots on opposite faces of a die always equals 7. A die rolls as shown below. At the starting point $(A)$ the top face is 3. Which will be the face at the end point $(B)$?
<image1>

image_dependency: question_text says 'A die rolls as shown below', so the roll path is specified visually
multi_step: question_text combines a rule ('opposite faces ... equals 7') with a visual roll sequence to determine the final face
solving requires tracking die orientation across movement from A to B, not just reading a single visible label
final answer depends on integrating text constraints and image-based spatial reasoning
verifiability: answer_type is numeric/number and answer_text is a single value '6'
the die-opposite rule and roll sequence make the result checkable by explicit orientation tracing

decision_reasons: question, image, and answer assets are all present with no registry issues
high image dependency is supported by the question and available diagram, so the core semantics are preserved
good multi-step and verifiability scores indicate strong collection value and checkable correctness
